devices.role: Conveyor controller; Modbus RTU master to VFD1
devices.source: photo review/photos/wire_2.jpg (2026-07-11 — model label + MAC ID 5C:88:16:D8:E4:D7 read directly); plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md; plc/ccw/controller/Controller/LogicalValues.csv

devices.note: GS10 control is HYBRID (full-res photo 2026-07-11): the top run/DI connector (FWD/REV/DI3-5/+24V/DCM) is EMPTY — run/dir/speed command is Modbus (RJ45 landed); the bottom analog/output connector (+10V/ACM/AI/AO1/DO1/DOC/PE) is WIRED, likely analog speed reference + feedback. Map = OI-22. Input is 230 V single-phase (2-wire), fed through the MLC NO contacts (E-003). Output to motor = 230 V 3-phase (motor is 230 V, technician-confirmed 2026-07-11, review/PHOTO_EVIDENCE_V7.md §3 — not PHOTO_EVIDENCE_V6.md, which predates this confirmation; standard 230 V 1φ-in / 230 V 3φ-out GS10).
devices.role: Drives conveyor motor. Power input is 230 V single-phase (R/L1, S/L2; T/L3 unused — technician-confirmed 2026-07-11, see E-003). Commanded over Modbus RTU (RJ45, Ch2 node 1) — photo confirms the RS-485 cable is landed. NOTE the GS10 control connector is ALSO wired (hybrid, not Modbus-only) — see OI-22.
devices.source: photo review/photos/wire_4.jpg (2026-07-11 — keypad, control-terminal legend, RJ45 read directly); plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md; plc/Prog_init_ConvSimple_v2.1.st; review/photos/gs10_full.jpg + gs10_control_terminals.jpg (2026-07-11 — full-resolution control-terminal read)

devices.role: 2-pole branch breaker on the 230 V single-phase supply (existence/type/rating unconfirmed)

devices.model: Schneider Electric TeSys CA3KN22BD (control relay, 2 NO + 2 NC, 24 VDC coil)
devices.note: CORRECTED V4 from photo: the earlier "safety POWER contactor / power poles on E-003" was an inference error — the physical 'MLC' device is a TeSys CA3K CONTROL RELAY (aux contacts only, no main poles). Coil A1/A2 + 24 VDC now verified. Aux-contact destinations = FIELD VERIFY (OI-26). A separate 3-phase motor contactor upstream of the GS10 = FIELD VERIFY (OI-21; none observed in the 2026-07-11 photos). Full-res photo confirms coil A1/A2 wired and NO contacts 13-14 & 43-44 in use (NC 21-22/31-32 appear unused); contact destinations FIELD VERIFY (OI-26). V6: MLC is the single-phase drive-supply contactor (technician-confirmed 2026-07-11), not out of the power path — reverses the V4 removal; device is still a CA3K control relay used within its contact rating for the small drive (P00.01=1.60 A). CR-2: Schneider TeSys CA3KN22BD catalog rating: Ith = 10 A conventional thermal current, classed for AC-15/DC-13 pilot (control-circuit) duty — NOT a power-circuit-rated contactor. Used well within its thermal rating for the ~1.6 A drive; OI: confirm suitability for the switched inrush (see OI-26, aux-contact destinations).
devices.role: Control relay (Schneider CA3KN22BD, hand-labeled 'MLC') USED AS THE VFD SUPPLY SWITCH — its two NO contacts 13-14 & 43-44 switch the 230 V single-phase supply into the GS10 (R/L1, S/L2); coil A1(+)/A2(-) 24 VDC driven by PLC O-02. Energizing O-02 closes the contacts and powers the drive. Shown on E-003 (power contacts) + E-006 (coil). NC 21-22/31-32 unused.
devices.sheet_label: Q1/MLC — Schneider TeSys CA3KN22BD control relay (24 VDC coil)
devices.source: photo review/photos/wire_3.jpg (2026-07-11 — device label 'MLC', part no CA3KN22BD, coil A1/A2 24V, aux 13-14/21-22/31-32/43-44 read directly); plc/CCW_VARIABLES_v4.0.txt:80 (O-02 coil); plc/Prog_init_ConvSimple_v2.1.st:214 (vfd_run_permit reads O-02); review/photos/mlc_full.jpg + mlc_top_terminals.jpg + mlc_bottom_terminals.jpg (2026-07-11 — full-resolution terminal read)

serial_config.note: Older GS10_Integration_Guide.md said 8N2 (P09.04=13) - SUPERSEDED by bench-verified 8N1.
serial_config.oi20_note: Line params pending re-verify (OI-20): 2026-05-20 export read 38.4k/8N2; 2026-05-26 bench sniff read 9600/8N1 (shown). Fresh keypad/readback to adjudicate.

troubleshooting: Nothing after config -> swap 485+/485- (A/B polarity; vendors disagree).
CRC errors -> usually polarity. Complete silence -> usually baud mismatch (confirm 9600 8N1).
GS10 CE10 / F30 comm-timeout -> no Modbus packets; check wiring, baud, P09.03 timeout.
120 ohm termination across SG+/SG- at the GS10 end for long runs (bench <2 m usually fine).
SGND is a dedicated conductor, not the shield; missing common -> intermittent corruption.

open_items.item: PS1 enclosure/chassis ground and DC-0V-to-PE bonding not documented — is PS1's DIN-rail/enclosure bonded to PE, and is the 24 VDC 0V rail (DB1 0V-bus) intentionally tied to PE anywhere (SELV/PELV grounding scheme), or left floating?
open_items.verify: Meter PS1 enclosure/DIN rail to the PE bus for continuity; meter DB1 0V-bus to the PE bus for continuity/resistance; confirm whether a deliberate 0V-PE bond exists or the 24 VDC system is intentionally floating.

notes: LINE-STYLE LEGEND, red: red marks EITHER FIELD-VERIFY status (a dashed, unverified conductor/device or an unverified wire-number tag) OR a safety-critical prohibition (a NOT/never instruction inside a sheet's SAFETY block, e.g. 'a monitored input is NOT a safety stop') — same color, two distinct meanings distinguished by context (dashed line vs. SAFETY-block bullet). This is intentional package-wide convention, not per-sheet drift.
safety: LOTO + wait ≥5 min for DC-bus discharge before touching (WI p.2 §2).
Monitored e-stop is NOT a safety stop — hardwire S0 to remove drive power per NFPA 79 / EN 60204-1 (style law §7; docs/references/industrial-wiring-diagram-standards.md).

sheets.scope: Cover / legend / device schedule / wire-numbering key / sheet index
sheets.title: Cover / legend / device schedule

caveat: Summary sheet only — no wire numbers are assigned here (see the wire-numbering key on E-001). For per-conductor evidence and FIELD VERIFY status, use E-003 (VFD power) and E-004 (24 VDC control power).
notes: Conductor-count ticks (e.g. 2 ticks = '1φ, 2W') mark circuit conductor count only — not a conduit/cable schedule.
Supply is 230 V SINGLE-PHASE (2-wire), technician-confirmed 2026-07-11 — this sheet corrects the earlier 3-phase assumption.
PS1 branch is drawn as a tap off the same incoming supply; the exact AC leg/circuit feeding PS1 is FIELD VERIFY (OI-25).

sheets.scope: Single-line summary of the CV-101 power distribution — one line per circuit, device symbols, conductor-count tick marks, NO per-conductor wire numbers. Map between E-003 (VFD power detail) and E-004 (24 VDC control power detail); not a substitute for either.
sheets.subtitle: 230 V 1φ supply → CB1 → Q1/MLC → VFD1 (GS10) → M1 · branch tap → PS1 (24 VDC control, E-004)
sheets.title: Power one-line

caveat: Supply is CONFIRMED 230 V single-phase (technician-confirmed 2026-07-11, review/PHOTO_EVIDENCE_V7.md) — R/L1, S/L2 only; T/L3 unused. Only the GS10 exact model/frame, breaker rating, and wire gauge remain NOT DOCUMENTED — every conductor FIELD VERIFY. P00.01 = 1.60 A (2026-05-20 export) is the only sizing clue.

MLC (Q1) is a control relay USED AS the single-phase drive-supply switch — its 2 NO contacts (13-14, 43-44) carry the 230 V 1φ supply into the GS10; coil A1/A2 from O-02 (E-006). Energizing O-02 powers the drive (technician-confirmed 2026-07-11).
notes: CB1 REQUIRED per GS10_UM L1758-1759 — type/rating unknown (OI-15).
M1: swap any two motor leads to reverse — GS10_UM L1773-1776.
VFD control source = Modbus — see E-007. No control wiring on this sheet.
Supply is 230 V SINGLE-PHASE (2-wire); drive output = 230 V 3φ to a 230 V motor (technician-confirmed 2026-07-11).

GS10 manual also warns against cycling a POWER-CIRCUIT switching device for normal run/stop — emergency situations only (GS10_UM.txt L1754-1757), same caution as L1811-1813 above. This applies to Q1/MLC (the device that powers the drive) exactly as it applies to CB1: normal run/stop is via Modbus (E-007), never by cycling Q1's coil.
safety: Never start/stop via input power (L1811-1813); CB1 is branch protection only, not a run/stop device.
LOTO + wait ≥5 min for DC-bus discharge before touching (WI p.2 §2).
Monitored e-stop is NOT a safety stop — hardwire S0 to remove drive power per NFPA 79 / EN 60204-1.
PE resistance ≤0.1Ω (L1760-61); shield/conduit bonded both ends (L1792-95); RFI jumper in/out FIELD VERIFY (L1693-1718, OI-17).
sources: plc/GS10_UM.txt (terminals L1971-1986; topology L1750-1813) · plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md (Q1 phase-5 test)

review/PHOTO_EVIDENCE_V7.md (technician confirmation 2026-07-11 — 230 V motor output, OI-27 resolved)

sheets.lineage: terminals per GS10 UM 1st Ed Rev B
sheets.scope: Upstream supply → branch protection (CB1) → Q1/MLC (single-phase drive-supply switch, 2 NO contacts) → VFD1 power input (R/L1, S/L2; T/L3 unused, 1φ) → motor output (U/T1, V/T2, W/T3) → motor M1; PE bonding. Q1/MLC's NO contacts 13-14 & 43-44 ARE the power path between CB1 and VFD1 (2026-07-11 technician field correction — reverses the earlier "no motor contactor, CB1 feeds VFD1 directly" reading; OI-21 RESOLVED).
sheets.subtitle: supply → CB1 → Q1/MLC (supply switch) → VFD1 (GS10) → M1 · 230 V 1φ in → 230 V 3φ to motor
sheets.title: VFD power (CB1 -> VFD1 -> M1)

caveat: PS1 is a verified Mean Well DIN-rail supply (24 V / 1.0 A out, 100-240 VAC / 0.55 A in) — nameplate read directly (photo review/photos/wire_2.jpg, 2026-07-11). DB1 polarity (blue vs white = +24V vs 0V), circuit count, PS1 branch fusing, and PS1's AC input source (which leg/circuit feeds L, N) are FIELD VERIFY (OI-25).
notes: DB1 is a push-in (WAGO-style), 2-level distribution block fed from PS1's +V/-V output; a bare-copper jumper bridges its top-left terminals in the photo (review/photos/dc_block_full.jpg) — internal jumpering/circuit count not documented.
PS1's DC-OK terminal (LED/relay status output) and PE input terminal are shown for reference only; neither carries a modeled conductor on this sheet.
PS1 enclosure/chassis ground and whether DC-0V is bonded to PE anywhere (SELV/PELV scheme vs intentionally floating) is NOT documented — FIELD VERIFY (OI-28).
+24V / 0V downstream of DB1 feed the E-005 input rail (W24/W0V) and the E-006 output bank 0 feed/return (W600/W609) — same electrical nodes, different sheet.
safety: PS1 is fed from AC mains — LOTO + de-energize before opening the panel or metering DB1 (WI p.2 §2).
Monitored e-stop is NOT a safety stop; de-energizing DB1/PS1 does not remove drive power — see E-003 for the drive supply isolation point.
sources: photo review/photos/wire_2.jpg (2026-07-11 — Mean Well nameplate read directly)
photo review/photos/dc_block_full.jpg + wire_1.jpg (2026-07-11 — DC distribution block)

sheets.scope: AC supply → PS1 (Mean Well 24 VDC / 1.0 A DIN supply, verified device) → DB1 (+/- push-in distribution block, verified to exist) → +24V / 0V control power consumed as PS1.+24V / PS1.0V on E-005 (PLC digital inputs) and E-006 (PLC outputs) — same electrical nodes, different sheet.
sheets.subtitle: AC → PS1 (Mean Well 24V/1A) → DB1 +/- distribution block → +24V/0V to E-005, E-006 loads
sheets.title: 24 VDC control power distribution (PS1)

notes: READS (acceptance): +24 VDC starts at PS1 (E-004) → device contact shown → proposed wire # → PLC terminal I-0x → function/OPC tag → returns via COM0 → 0V (E-004). Verified vs field-verify marked per conductor.

VERIFIED (solid) = PLC terminal + function traced to the running program / Ignition OPC docs; FIELD VERIFY (dashed) = physical wiring not documented — meter it (see E-009 open items). Red is also used inside the SAFETY block above for a NOT/never prohibition (e.g. 'a monitored input is NOT a safety stop') — same color, a different meaning by context (see E-001 LINE-STYLE LEGEND note).
COM0 input common — sink/source jumpering FIELD VERIFY (OI-02).
safety: I-02/I-03 are MONITORED e-stop inputs only — a monitored input is NOT a safety stop. A compliant install must hardwire S0 to remove drive power (NFPA 79 / EN 60204-1, stop cat 0/1) (style law §7; docs/references/industrial-wiring-diagram-standards.md).

sheets.scope: Micro820 I-00..I-05 (+ spares I-06..I-11); E-stop, direction selector, run PB, photo-eye
sheets.title: PLC digital inputs

caveat: Hardwired fallback documented in MIRA-WI-001 v3 §3 Fig 1 (DO_03..DO_07 → GS10 DI1..DI5 + DCM) — NOT ACTIVE and NOT drawn: P02.0x at factory default per the 2026-05-20 parameter export; DO_07 does not exist on the 2080-LC20-20QBB (O-00..O-06); DO_03 collides with PBRunLED in the live I/O map. Bench presence of any such wiring = FIELD VERIFY (OI-18).
notes: Q1/MLC = Schneider TeSys CA3KN22BD control relay (2 NO + 2 NC, 24 VDC coil); coil A1(+)/A2(-) driven by O-02 (verified from photo wire_3). NO contacts 13-14/43-44 switch the VFD's 230 V 1φ supply (see E-003); 21-22/31-32 NC unused.

GS10 control (full-res photo): run/dir/speed = Modbus — top FWD/REV/DI connector is EMPTY, RJ45 landed. Bottom analog/output row (+10V/ACM/AI/AO1/DO1/DOC) is wired — likely speed reference + feedback. Map = OI-22.
NO contacts 13-14 & 43-44 switch the 230 V 1φ VFD supply (see E-003); coil A1/A2 from O-02. NC 21-22/31-32 unused.
GS10 run/dir/freq = Modbus — see E-007 (P00.20=1, P00.21=2 per 2026-05-20 parameter export)
No as-built: O-00/O-01/O-03 from CCW v4.0; O-02 corroborated by live Prog_init v2.1.
Output bank technology + common feed = FIELD VERIFY (OI-09): WI-001 p.4 says relay dry contacts; ±CM polarity naming suggests DC-fed banks — conflicting evidence, resolve per 2080-IN009 + meter.

GS10 manual warns against cycling a power-circuit switching device for normal run/stop — emergency situations only (GS10_UM.txt L1754-1757, L1811-1813). This applies to Q1/MLC (energized by O-02, switches the drive's 230 V 1φ supply — see E-003): O-02/Q1 stays energized during normal operation; run/stop is commanded via Modbus (E-007), never by cycling Q1.

S2 lamp (this sheet, O-03/PBRunLED) and S2's run/rearm pushbutton contact (E-005, I-04) are two elements of the SAME physical device — same cross-reference symmetry as Q1/MLC's coil (this sheet) ↔ power contacts (E-003).
safety: Monitored outputs are NOT a safety function.
E-stop must remove power per NFPA 79.

Q1/MLC is a CONTROL RELAY (24 VDC coil, driven by O-02) whose 2 NO contacts (13-14, 43-44) switch the drive's 230 V 1φ supply (V6 correction, E-003) — de-energizing O-02 DOES remove Q1's switched supply to the GS10. Q1 is NOT an NFPA-79/EN-60204-1 safety-rated disconnect (small control-relay contacts, not a lockable safety contactor); the e-stop (S0) → O-02 relationship is FIELD VERIFY (OI-26). Do not treat this circuit as the LOTO isolation point — always LOTO at CB1 or upstream.
O-02 is the sole MLC/Q1 drive-enable coil output — do not reuse it for any other load (MIRA_PLC_WorkInstruction_v3.pdf §3: "DO_02 is already the MLC drive-enable coil and must never be reused").

sheets.lineage: output map CCW v4.0 + live Prog_init v2.1; O-02 do-not-reuse (MIRA_PLC_WorkInstruction_v3.pdf §3) — see SAFETY
sheets.note: Hardwired fallback documented in MIRA-WI-001 v3 §3 Fig 1 (DO_03..DO_07 → GS10 DI1..DI5 + DCM) — NOT ACTIVE and NOT drawn: P02.0x at factory default per 2026-05-20 parameter export; DO_07 does not exist on the 2080-LC20-20QBB (O-00..O-06); DO_03 collides with PBRunLED in the live I/O map. Bench presence of any such wiring = FIELD VERIFY (OI-18). Correction: July-3 target sketch assumed CR relay → GS10 DI — SUPERSEDED by P00.21=2 (RS-485 Modbus) + ContactorQ1 evidence. NOTE (2026-07-11): photo evidence shows the GS10 control-terminal connector IS populated on the bench — control is HYBRID Modbus + hardwired I/O; the specific WI-001 DO_03..DO_07 fallback pattern remains unconfirmed (OI-18) but "Modbus-only" is no longer the working assumption — see OI-22.
sheets.scope: Micro820 outputs O-00..O-06 (banks +CM0/-CM0, +CM1/-CM1): PL1/PL2 pilots, Q1/MLC control-relay coil, S2 lamp; O-04..O-06 spare. GS10 run/dir/freq = Modbus (E-007, P00.21=2); the GS10 control-terminal connector is ALSO populated on the bench (hybrid, not Modbus-only — map = OI-22).
sheets.subtitle: O-00..O-06 · pilots, Q1/MLC control-relay coil, button lamp · GS10 control = HYBRID Modbus + hardwired I/O (E-007; map OI-22)

caveat: CORRECTED from May-16 draft:  Channel 0 → 2   ·   SGND pin 1/8 → pin 3   ·   8N2 → 8N1.  Do not copy the old values.
notes: Belden 3105A — shielded twisted pair (485+/485-) + 1 conductor (SGND) + drain
VERIFIED (solid) = recovered CommsToVFD + Beginner_Verify p48 + Prog_init v2.1; FIELD VERIFY (dashed) = exact PLC chassis ground point; cable P/N if not Belden 3105A.
Port locations per CommsToVFD (PLC top-edge connector; GS10 front-face RJ45) — WI-001 says bottom-front. Wayfinding only; confirm on unit (FIELD VERIFY).
GS10 RS-485 RJ45 cable confirmed landed (photo wire_4). The GS10 control connector (FWD/REV/DI/AI/AO1/DO1) is also wired — see OI-22.
GS10 RS-485 RJ45 confirmed landed (full-res photo). The GS10 top run/DI control connector is EMPTY — no hardwired run/dir; command is Modbus. Bottom analog/output row is wired (OI-22).
GS10 run/freq source = Modbus: P00.20=1, P00.21=2 (2026-05-20 export; same fact as E-006).
P09.09 (Response Delay) = 10.0 ms — program flags CRITICAL (default 2.0 ms → ErrorID-55): Prog_init_ConvSimple_v2.1.st:79.
120 ohm termination (SG+/SG- at GS10 end): often omitted on a <2 m bench run — presence is FIELD VERIFY, not confirmed here.

sheets.note: Values reconciled to bench-verified: Channel 2 (not 0), SGND=pin 3 (not 1/8), 8N1 (not 8N2). Mostly VERIFIED (CommsToVFD + Beginner_Verify p48 + Prog_init v2.1); shield chassis point = field-verify.
sheets.scope: Modbus-only. PLC1 embedded RS-485 (Ch2) D+/D-/SG/shield <-> VFD1 GS10 RJ45 pin5/4/3. NO FWD/REV/VI/ACM/FA.
sheets.title: RS-485 Modbus (PLC1 Ch2 <-> VFD1 RJ45)

notes: X1 (field terminal strip, devices.yaml) is where field devices land before the PLC. Its physical terminal-strip layout and numbering are NOT documented in any recovered source — FIELD VERIFY. This table lists the conductors on each side of it, indexed by their owning sheet — it is not X1's internal terminal map.
Rows are sorted by sheet, then wire number/label. VERIFIED (black) vs FIELD VERIFY (red) mirrors each conductor's status on its owning sheet — see that sheet for the drawn circuit and evidence.
